C[SunPin: ""Cool in Siberia."", He says. :-) I saw a documentary recently called ""Happy People"" about life in the Taiga. That looks like the toughest living possible.

C[SunPin: Black was far more charitable than I expected.
SunPin: The simple moves are the best. Complicated fights leave too much to chance. I want to force black to resign. That means making good moves. Dansu.

C[tordo: still stuck, you need to kill P 3,4,16
C[tordo: sorry, you also need to kill P16 and L13
C[tordo: kill P16, L13, and block at R1, or we can't score.
C[SunPin: In winter… White f15. Black h14. White must then decide what you would like to do. Lose the white stone at J14 or fill at F16?
SunPin: In spring, o16 will cause white to lose a point of territory. As will p13.
SunPin: In summer… Try to get p5.
SunPin: In autumn… This is interesting…h9 for black. A few variations here. Sente at f10. The response must be e9 for white. If g9 is available, the white group dies.
C[SunPin: My thoughts on winter are going according to plan. Black is in double Atari. Lose one stone at j14 or a bunch of stones on the left.
C[SunPin: Unless my main comrade has some nifty trick up his sleeve, then K7 was the game-winning move.
C[SunPin: At one move per day, this was a battle worthy of the gods. :-) Thank you very much.
